core_mechanism: a quantum phase transition driven by the competition between two opposing energy scales, where tuning the ratio of the two coupling constants (a control parameter) drives the system across a critical threshold separating two qualitatively distinct ordered phases
brief_reason: The abstract states the phenomena stem from the competition between single-electron charging energy and Josephson coupling energy, with a superconductor-insulator transition occurring at a quantum critical point as the ratio of these coupling constants (and external fields) is varied.
